title: ophiorrhiza ridleyi
intro: Ophiorrhiza ridleyi, also known as the Asian swamp tea or wild tea, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating cough and cold symptoms, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ophiorrhiza ridleyi is often used in teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as in traditional remedies for treating various health conditions.
